name: Qwen-Agent
tagline: Build LLM agents on Qwen with tool calling, MCP, code interpreter, and RAG
seoTitle: Qwen-Agent: Build LLM Agents on Qwen Models
metaDescription: Qwen-Agent is an open-source Python framework for building agents on Qwen models, with function calling, MCP, a code interpreter, RAG, and a Gradio GUI.
overview: Qwen-Agent is an open-source framework from the Qwen team at Alibaba for building LLM applications on top of Qwen models. It builds on the models' instruction following, tool usage, planning, and memory abilities, and ships example apps such as a Browser Assistant, a Code Interpreter, and a Custom Assistant. The same framework serves as the backend of Qwen Chat.

The framework gives you atomic building blocks (LLM classes that support function calling and Tool classes you can subclass) plus higher-level Agent classes like the ready-made Assistant, which can call tools and read files. You connect it to a model either through Alibaba Cloud's DashScope API or your own OpenAI-compatible service such as vLLM or Ollama.

Beyond plain chat, it includes a built-in code interpreter that runs code inside a local Docker sandbox, support for the Model Context Protocol (MCP) so agents can use external tool servers, a RAG solution aimed at very long documents, and an optional Gradio-based web UI for quickly demoing an agent.
features: Function calling (tool calling) on Qwen models, including parallel, multi-step, and multi-turn calls
Ready-made agents such as the Assistant, which can use tools and read files (for example PDFs)
Built-in code interpreter that executes generated code in a local Docker sandbox
MCP support, so agents can connect to Model Context Protocol tool servers (memory, filesystem, SQLite, and more)
RAG support for question-answering over very long documents, up to around 1M-token contexts
Optional Gradio-based GUI to launch a web demo of your agent in a few lines

gettingStarted.intro: Install the package from PyPI with the optional extras you need, point it at a Qwen model service, then create an Assistant agent and run it. The example below mirrors the project README.

steps.heading: Install Qwen-Agent
steps.body: Install the stable release from PyPI. The optional extras in double brackets add GUI, RAG, code interpreter, and MCP support; use the bare package for minimal requirements.

steps.heading: Connect a model service
steps.body: Use Alibaba Cloud DashScope by setting your DASHSCOPE_API_KEY environment variable, or deploy your own OpenAI-compatible service with vLLM or Ollama and point the config at its base URL.

steps.heading: Create and run an Assistant agent
steps.body: Configure the LLM, create an Assistant with a system message, an optional tool list, and files to read, then stream its responses. code_interpreter is a built-in tool.

steps.heading: Launch a web UI (optional)
steps.body: Wrap your agent in the Gradio-based WebUI to get a chat interface for quick demos. The GUI requires Python 3.10 or higher.

useCases: Build a custom assistant that reads your documents and calls your own tools to answer questions
Run an agent that writes and executes code in a sandbox to analyze data or process files
Connect Qwen models to external MCP tool servers (filesystem, SQLite, memory) for richer automation
Do retrieval-augmented question-answering over very long documents using the built-in RAG solution
